name: grafana
description: A Pulumi package for creating and managing grafana cloud resources.
attribution: This Pulumi package is based on the [`grafana` Terraform Provider](https://github.com/terraform-providers/terraform-provider-grafana).

nodejs.packageDescription: A Pulumi package for creating and managing grafana cloud resources.
nodejs.readme:
> This provider is a derived work of the [Terraform Provider](https://github.com/terraform-providers/terraform-provider-grafana)
> distributed under [MPL 2.0](https://www.mozilla.org/en-US/MPL/2.0/). If you encounter a bug or missing feature,
> first check the [`pulumi-grafana` repo](https://github.com/pulumiverse/pulumi-grafana/issues); however, if that doesn't turn up anything,
> please consult the source [`terraform-provider-grafana` repo](https://github.com/terraform-providers/terraform-provider-grafana/issues).

python.readme:
> This provider is a derived work of the [Terraform Provider](https://github.com/terraform-providers/terraform-provider-grafana)
> distributed under [MPL 2.0](https://www.mozilla.org/en-US/MPL/2.0/). If you encounter a bug or missing feature,
> first check the [`pulumi-grafana` repo](https://github.com/pulumiverse/pulumi-grafana/issues); however, if that doesn't turn up anything,
> please consult the source [`terraform-provider-grafana` repo](https://github.com/terraform-providers/terraform-provider-grafana/issues).

auth.description: API token or basic auth `username:password`. May alternatively be set via the `GRAFANA_AUTH` environment variable.

caCert.description:
Certificate CA bundle to use to verify the Grafana server's certificate. May alternatively be set via the
`GRAFANA_CA_CERT` environment variable.

cloudApiKey.description: API key for Grafana Cloud. May alternatively be set via the `GRAFANA_CLOUD_API_KEY` environment variable.

cloudApiUrl.description: Grafana Cloud's API URL. May alternatively be set via the `GRAFANA_CLOUD_API_URL` environment variable.

httpHeaders.description:
Optional. HTTP headers mapping keys to values used for accessing the Grafana API. May alternatively be set via the
`GRAFANA_HTTP_HEADERS` environment variable in JSON format.

insecureSkipVerify.description: Skip TLS certificate verification. May alternatively be set via the `GRAFANA_INSECURE_SKIP_VERIFY` environment variable.

oncallAccessToken.description: A Grafana OnCall access token. May alternatively be set via the `GRAFANA_ONCALL_ACCESS_TOKEN` environment variable.

oncallUrl.description: An Grafana OnCall backend address. May alternatively be set via the `GRAFANA_ONCALL_URL` environment variable.

orgId.description:
The organization id to operate on within grafana. May alternatively be set via the `GRAFANA_ORG_ID` environment
variable.

retries.description:
The amount of retries to use for Grafana API calls. May alternatively be set via the `GRAFANA_RETRIES` environment
variable.

smAccessToken.description: A Synthetic Monitoring access token. May alternatively be set via the `GRAFANA_SM_ACCESS_TOKEN` environment variable.

smUrl.description:
Synthetic monitoring backend address. May alternatively be set via the `GRAFANA_SM_URL` environment variable. The
correct value for each service region is cited in the [Synthetic Monitoring
documentation](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana-cloud/synthetic-monitoring/private-probes/#probe-api-server-url). Note
the `sm_url` value is optional, but it must correspond with the value specified as the `region_slug` in the
`grafana_cloud_stack` resource. Also note that when a Terraform configuration contains multiple provider instances
managing SM resources associated with the same Grafana stack, specifying an explicit `sm_url` set to the same value for
each provider ensures all providers interact with the same SM API.

storeDashboardSha256.description: Set to true if you want to save only the sha256sum instead of complete dashboard model JSON in the tfstate.

tlsCert.description:
Client TLS certificate file to use to authenticate to the Grafana server. May alternatively be set via the
`GRAFANA_TLS_CERT` environment variable.

tlsKey.description:
Client TLS key file to use to authenticate to the Grafana server. May alternatively be set via the `GRAFANA_TLS_KEY`
environment variable.

url.description: The root URL of a Grafana server. May alternatively be set via the `GRAFANA_URL` environment variable.

provider.description:
The provider type for the grafana package. By default, resources use package-wide configuration
settings, however an explicit `Provider` instance may be created and passed during resource
construction to achieve fine-grained programmatic control over provider settings. See the
[documentation](https://www.pulumi.com/docs/reference/programming-model/#providers) for more information.

disableResolveMessage.description: Whether to disable sending resolve messages.

frequency.description: Frequency of alert reminders. Frequency must be set if reminders are enabled.

isDefault.description: Is this the default channel for all your alerts.

name.description: The name of the alert notification channel.

secureSettings.description:
Additional secure settings, for full reference lookup [Grafana Supported Settings
documentation](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/administration/provisioning/#supported-settings).

sendReminder.description: Whether to send reminders for triggered alerts.

settings.description:
Additional settings, for full reference see [Grafana HTTP API

type.description: The type of the alert notification channel.

cloudStackSlug.description:
If set, the API key will be created for the given Cloud stack. This can be used to bootstrap a management API key for a
new stack. **Note**: This requires a cloud token to be configured.

If set, the API key will be created for the given Cloud stack. This can be used to bootstrap a management API key for a
new stack. **Note**: This requires a cloud token to be configured.

stateInputs.description: Input properties used for looking up and filtering ApiKey resources.

cloudStackSlug.description:
If set, the API key will be created for the given Cloud stack. This can be used to bootstrap a management API key for a
new stack. **Note**: This requires a cloud token to be configured.

builtinRole.description: Organization roles (`Viewer`, `Editor`, `Admin`) or `Grafana Admin` to assign the roles to.

roles.description: Fixed or custom roles which provide granular access for specific resources within Grafana.

cloudOrgSlug.description: The slug of the organization to create the API key in. This is the same slug as the organization name in the URL.

name.description: Name of the API key.

role.description:
Role of the API key. Should be one of [Viewer Editor Admin MetricsPublisher PluginPublisher]. See
https://grafana.com/docs/grafana-cloud/api/#create-api-key for details.

stackSlug.description: The stack id to which the plugin should be installed.

alertmanagerName.description: Name of the Alertmanager instance configured for this stack.

alertmanagerStatus.description: Status of the Alertmanager instance configured for this stack.

alertmanagerUrl.description: Base URL of the Alertmanager instance configured for this stack.

alertmanagerUserId.description: User ID of the Alertmanager instance configured for this stack.

name.description: Name of stack. Conventionally matches the url of the instance (e.g. “<stack_slug>.grafana.net”).

orgId.description: Organization id to assign to this stack.

orgName.description: Organization name to assign to this stack.

orgSlug.description: Organization slug to assign to this stack.

prometheusRemoteEndpoint.description: Use this URL to query hosted metrics data e.g. Prometheus data source in Grafana

prometheusRemoteWriteEndpoint.description: Use this URL to send prometheus metrics to Grafana cloud

prometheusUserId.description: Promehteus user ID. Used for e.g. remote_write.

regionSlug.description:
Region slug to assign to this stack. Changing region will destroy the existing stack and create a new one in the desired
region

slug.description:
Subdomain that the Grafana instance will be available at (i.e. setting slug to “<stack_slug>” will make the instance
available at “https://<stack_slug>.grafana.net".

url.description: Custom URL for the Grafana instance. Must have a CNAME setup to point to `.grafana.net` before creating the stack

waitForReadiness.description:
Whether to wait for readiness of the stack after creating it. The check is a HEAD request to the stack URL (Grafana
instance).

waitForReadinessTimeout.description: How long to wait for readiness (if enabled).

alertmanagers.description: A contact point that sends notifications to other Alertmanager instances.

dingdings.description: A contact point that sends notifications to DingDing.

discords.description: A contact point that sends notifications as Discord messages

emails.description: A contact point that sends notifications to an email address.

googlechats.description: A contact point that sends notifications to Google Chat.

kafkas.description: A contact point that publishes notifications to Apache Kafka topics.

name.description: The name of the contact point.

opsgenies.description: A contact point that sends notifications to OpsGenie.

pagerduties.description: A contact point that sends notifications to PagerDuty.

pushovers.description: A contact point that sends notifications to Pushover.

sensugos.description: A contact point that sends notifications to SensuGo.

slacks.description: A contact point that sends notifications to Slack.

teams.description: A contact point that sends notifications to Microsoft Teams.

telegrams.description: A contact point that sends notifications to Telegram.

threemas.description: A contact point that sends notifications to Threema.

victorops.description: A contact point that sends notifications to VictorOps (now known as Splunk OnCall).

webhooks.description:
A contact point that sends notifications to an arbitrary webhook, using the Prometheus webhook format defined here:
https://prometheus.io/docs/alerting/latest/configuration/#webhook_config

wecoms.description: A contact point that sends notifications to WeCom.

dashboardId.description: The numeric ID of the dashboard computed by Grafana.

folder.description: The id of the folder to save the dashboard in. This attribute is a string to reflect the type of the folder's id.

message.description: Set a commit message for the version history.

overwrite.description:
Set to true if you want to overwrite existing dashboard with newer version, same dashboard title in folder or same
dashboard uid.

slug.description: URL friendly version of the dashboard title. This field is deprecated, please use `uid` instead.

uid.description:
The unique identifier of a dashboard. This is used to construct its URL. It's automatically generated if not provided
when creating a dashboard. The uid allows having consistent URLs for accessing dashboards and when syncing dashboards
between multiple Grafana installs.

url.description: The full URL of the dashboard.

version.description:
Whenever you save a version of your dashboard, a copy of that version is saved so that previous versions of your
dashboard are not lost.

folder.description: The id of the folder to save the dashboard in. This attribute is a string to reflect the type of the folder's id.

message.description: Set a commit message for the version history.

overwrite.description:
Set to true if you want to overwrite existing dashboard with newer version, same dashboard title in folder or same
dashboard uid.

stateInputs.description: Input properties used for looking up and filtering Dashboard resources.

dashboardId.description: The numeric ID of the dashboard computed by Grafana.

folder.description: The id of the folder to save the dashboard in. This attribute is a string to reflect the type of the folder's id.

message.description: Set a commit message for the version history.

overwrite.description:
Set to true if you want to overwrite existing dashboard with newer version, same dashboard title in folder or same
dashboard uid.

slug.description: URL friendly version of the dashboard title. This field is deprecated, please use `uid` instead.

uid.description:
The unique identifier of a dashboard. This is used to construct its URL. It's automatically generated if not provided
when creating a dashboard. The uid allows having consistent URLs for accessing dashboards and when syncing dashboards
between multiple Grafana installs.

url.description: The full URL of the dashboard.

version.description:
Whenever you save a version of your dashboard, a copy of that version is saved so that previous versions of your
dashboard are not lost.

dashboardId.description: ID of the dashboard to apply permissions to.

permissions.description: The permission items to add/update. Items that are omitted from the list will be removed.

dashboardId.description: ID of the dashboard to apply permissions to.

permissions.description: The permission items to add/update. Items that are omitted from the list will be removed.

stateInputs.description: Input properties used for looking up and filtering DashboardPermission resources.

dashboardId.description: ID of the dashboard to apply permissions to.

permissions.description: The permission items to add/update. Items that are omitted from the list will be removed.

accessMode.description: The method by which Grafana will access the data source: `proxy` or `direct`.

basicAuthEnabled.description: Whether to enable basic auth for the data source.

basicAuthPassword.description:
Basic auth password. Deprecated: Use secure_json_data.basic_auth_password instead. This attribute is removed in Grafana
9.0+.
basicAuthPassword.deprecationMessage: Use secure_json_data.basic_auth_password instead. This attribute is removed in Grafana 9.0+.

databaseName.description: (Required by some data source types) The name of the database to use on the selected data source server.

isDefault.description: Whether to set the data source as default. This should only be `true` to a single data source.

name.description: A unique name for the data source.

password.description:
(Required by some data source types) The password to use to authenticate to the data source. Deprecated: Use
secure_json_data.password instead. This attribute is removed in Grafana 9.0+.
password.deprecationMessage: Use secure_json_data.password instead. This attribute is removed in Grafana 9.0+.

type.description: The data source type. Must be one of the supported data source keywords.

The unique identifier (UID) of a library panel uniquely identifies library panels between multiple Grafana installs.
It’s automatically generated unless you specify it during library panel creation.The UID provides consistent URLs for
accessing library panels and when syncing library panels between multiple Grafana installs.

groupBies.description:
A list of alert labels to group alerts into notifications by. Use the special label `...` to group alerts by all labels,
effectively disabling grouping.

groupInterval.description: Minimum time interval between two notifications for the same group. Default is 5 minutes.

groupWait.description: Time to wait to buffer alerts of the same group before sending a notification. Default is 30 seconds.

policies.description: Routing rules for specific label sets.

repeatInterval.description: Minimum time interval for re-sending a notification if an alert is still firing. Default is 4 hours.

adminUser.description:
The login name of the configured default admin user for the Grafana installation. If unset, this value defaults to
admin, the Grafana default. Grafana adds the default admin user to all organizations automatically upon creation, and
this parameter keeps Terraform from removing it from organizations.

admins.description:
A list of email addresses corresponding to users who should be given admin access to the organization. Note: users
specified here must already exist in Grafana unless 'create_users' is set to true.

createUsers.description:
Whether or not to create Grafana users specified in the organization's membership if they don't already exist in
Grafana. If unspecified, this parameter defaults to true, creating placeholder users with the name, login, and email set
to the email of the user, and a random password. Setting this option to false will cause an error to be thrown for any
users that do not already exist in Grafana.

editors.description:
A list of email addresses corresponding to users who should be given editor access to the organization. Note: users
specified here must already exist in Grafana unless 'create_users' is set to true.

name.description: The display name for the Grafana organization created.

orgId.description: The organization id assigned to this organization by Grafana.

viewers.description:
A list of email addresses corresponding to users who should be given viewer access to the organization. Note: users
specified here must already exist in Grafana unless 'create_users' is set to true.

adminUser.description:
The login name of the configured default admin user for the Grafana installation. If unset, this value defaults to
admin, the Grafana default. Grafana adds the default admin user to all organizations automatically upon creation, and
this parameter keeps Terraform from removing it from organizations.

admins.description:
A list of email addresses corresponding to users who should be given admin access to the organization. Note: users
specified here must already exist in Grafana unless 'create_users' is set to true.

Grafana. If unspecified, this parameter defaults to true, creating placeholder users with the name, login, and email set
to the email of the user, and a random password. Setting this option to false will cause an error to be thrown for any
users that do not already exist in Grafana.

specified here must already exist in Grafana unless 'create_users' is set to true.

alertSensitivity.description:
Can be set to `none`, `low`, `medium`, or `high` to correspond to the check [alert
levels](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana-cloud/synthetic-monitoring/synthetic-monitoring-alerting/).

basicMetricsOnly.description:
Metrics are reduced by default. Set this to `false` if you'd like to publish all metrics. We maintain a [full list of
metrics](https://github.com/grafana/synthetic-monitoring-agent/tree/main/internal/scraper/testdata) collected for each.

frequency.description:
How often the check runs in milliseconds (the value is not truly a "frequency" but a "period"). The minimum acceptable
value is 1 second (1000 ms), and the maximum is 120 seconds (120000 ms).

labels.description:
Custom labels to be included with collected metrics and logs. The maximum number of labels that can be specified per
check is 5. These are applied, along with the probe-specific labels, to the outgoing metrics. The names and values of
the labels cannot be empty, and the maximum length is 32 bytes.

probes.description: List of probe location IDs where this target will be checked from.

settings.description: Check settings. Should contain exactly one nested block.

tenantId.description: The tenant ID of the check.

timeout.description:
Specifies the maximum running time for the check in milliseconds. The minimum acceptable value is 1 second (1000 ms),
and the maximum 10 seconds (10000 ms).

alertSensitivity.description:
Can be set to `none`, `low`, `medium`, or `high` to correspond to the check [alert
levels](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana-cloud/synthetic-monitoring/synthetic-monitoring-alerting/).

basicMetricsOnly.description:
Metrics are reduced by default. Set this to `false` if you'd like to publish all metrics. We maintain a [full list of
metrics](https://github.com/grafana/synthetic-monitoring-agent/tree/main/internal/scraper/testdata) collected for each.

timeout.description:
Specifies the maximum running time for the check in milliseconds. The minimum acceptable value is 1 second (1000 ms),
and the maximum 10 seconds (10000 ms).
